subject: Virology
title: Shingles: The Zoster Decode
hook: Shingles is one fact away from a wrong answer. Here is the zoster story examiners test.

points.heading: One virus, two diseases
bullets: Reactivation years later is herpes zoster.
Driven by waning cell-mediated immunity, mostly over 50.

points.heading: Latency in the dorsal root ganglia
points.icon: a dorsal root ganglion on a nerve
bullets: After chickenpox, VZV lies dormant in dorsal root ganglia.
It reactivates down one sensory nerve.
That makes zoster unilateral and dermatomal.

points.heading: Pain comes before the rash
bullets: Dermatomal pain or paraesthesia precedes vesicles by 2 to 3 days.
Rash is grouped vesicles on an erythematous base.
Zoster sine herpete: dermatomal pain, no rash.

points.heading: PHN: know the cutoff
bullets: PHN is pain over 3 months after rash onset.
Antivirals within 72 hours shorten attack, ease acute pain.
Their benefit for preventing PHN is limited.

captionLines: Shingles is one fact away from a wrong answer.
Dorsal root ganglia latency, a unilateral dermatomal rash, PHN at more than 3 months after the rash, and the 72-hour antiviral window.
Lock those four and you will not lose marks here. 🎯
